title: Rearrange String k Distance Apart (ANY_VALID variant: any valid arrangement)
stdin_format: Single line: a non-empty string s (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 10^6, consisting of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters and digits).
output_format:
A single line: either a valid rearrangement where no two adjacent characters are identical, or an empty string "" if no such rearrangement exists.

Canonical validator behavior (pseudo-code; actual implementation in main):
  1. Parse the returned string from the user's solution.
  2. Count character frequencies in both input and output.
  3. If frequencies don't match, output "INVALID".
  4. If any two adjacent characters in output are the same, output "INVALID".
  5. If output is a valid rearrangement, output the lexicographically smallest valid rearrangement of the input (deterministically computed; e.g., via greedy max-heap + alternation or equivalent).
  6. If input has no valid rearrangement, confirm output is "" and output "".
  7. If user's output is "", check whether a valid arrangement existed; if yes, output "INVALID"; if no, output "".

Expected output format: a string (possibly empty ""), or "INVALID" for invalid submissions.

test_cases.name: Edge: Maximum frequency exactly at boundary
Edge: Maximum frequency just over boundary
Stress: many characters with one dominating
